questions.prompt: Looking at the graph of a function, which of the following observations would indicate that the function is NOT continuous at x = 2?
options: A) The function has a sharp corner at the point (2, 3)
B) There is a jump discontinuity where the left-side limit and right-side limit are different values
C) The function is increasing rapidly near x = 2
D) The function passes through the point (2, 0)
questions.correctAnswer: B) There is a jump discontinuity where the left-side limit and right-side limit are different values
questions.explanation: A function is continuous at a point if three conditions are met: (1) the function is defined at that point, (2) the limit exists at that point, and (3) the function value equals the limit. A jump discontinuity—where the left-hand and right-hand limits differ—violates the second condition, making the function discontinuous. While a sharp corner (option A) can be continuous if the function value and both one-sided limits are equal, a jump clearly breaks continuity. Rapid increase and passing through specific points don't indicate discontinuity by themselves.
